title: Docker Container Port Mapping Validator Fixer

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate Docker container port mappings. Given a string of port mappings in the format 'container-port:host-port', validate if the host-port is a valid port number (0-32767). The function should return true if all host-ports are valid, and false otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string of port mappings in the format 'container-port:host-port', separated by commas.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether all host-ports are valid.
statement.notes: The function should not consider the container-port, only the host-port.

examples.explanation: The host-port 65538 is not a valid port number.

hints.text: Split the input string into individual port mappings.
For each port mapping, split it into container-port and host-port.
Check if the host-port is a valid port number.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The function should iterate over each port mapping, split it into container-port and host-port, and check if the host-port is a valid port number.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a for loop to iterate over each port mapping, and a conditional statement to check if the host-port is valid.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use a regular expression to split the input string into individual port mappings, and a list comprehension to check if each host-port is valid.
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use a single regular expression to split the input string into individual port mappings, and check if each host-port is valid in a single loop.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return true if all host-ports are valid, and false otherwise.
editorial.dryRun: You can test the function with the provided examples to see if it works correctly.
